strengths: You applied research skills exceptionally, consulting both primary sources (librarians, teen authors via interviews and emails) and secondary sources, and organized your inquiries with a structured research plan.
Your self-management and organization are evident through proactive use of a reminders app and a flexible, prioritized timetable, ensuring steady progress despite competing demands.

Creative and critical-thinking skills shine in your use of a traditional plot diagram to structure your narrative and in your cover design concepts, which use compelling visual metaphors and responsive typography for cross-device compatibility.
weaknesses: Although various ATL skills are illustrated with examples, explicitly linking each skill to how it improved the final productâs quality would deepen the impact of your evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You have authentically integrated a wide range of ATL skillsâresearch, communication, self-management, critical and creative thinkingâwith relevant, concrete examples. By articulating the direct effects of these skills on the productâs quality, you will strengthen the connection between process and outcome.

strengths: Your visual diagram effectively links your passion, target audience and desired impact to your learning goal, demonstrating strategic alignment of objectives.
The action plan table includes specific tasks, dates, durations and progress status, showing clear sequencing and thorough planning of project steps.
weaknesses: Your learning goal does not include a timeline or measurable milestones, making it less actionable and harder to monitor progress.
The plan lacks contingency measures and a detailed list of required resources, which would enhance feasibility and resilience to unexpected delays.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You have demonstrated strong strategic planning through visual mapping and a detailed action plan, which establishes clarity and direction. To make your plan more robust, embedding time-bound milestones and preparing for potential setbacks would improve its practicality and ensure sustained momentum.

strengths: You grounded your evaluation in user data by using survey feedback to assess target-audience alignment, lending credibility to your design decisions.

Your honest critique of accessibility limitations (translations, media optimization) and reflection on personal barriers (self-doubt, perfectionism) demonstrate deep metacognitive and information-literacy skills.
Seeking and incorporating peer feedback shows adaptability and an open-minded approach to improvement.
weaknesses: Your self-evaluation ratings, while justified, are not explicitly mapped back to the rubric descriptors, which would clarify how each criterion has been met.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection combines user-centered evaluation, critical self-awareness and responsiveness to feedback, showcasing mature reflective practice. To fully meet the highest standards, align your reflections more closely with the rubric descriptors so that each success criterion is explicitly addressed.

annotations.comment: Using survey feedback to rate target-audience alignment (4/5) shows you grounded your evaluation in user data, reinforcing your design decisions.
Your acknowledgement of accessibility weaknesses (lack of translations, non-optimized media) reflects honest self-evaluation and sets clear areas for growth.
Your excerpt demonstrates authentic voice through effective use of colloquialism and descriptive style, engaging your targeted teen readers.
Your reflection on personal barriers (self-doubt, perfectionism) shows deep metacognitive awareness of how emotions impact the writing process.
Your visual diagram linking passion, audience and desired impact into a learning goal shows strategic planning and alignment of objectives.
Your self-evaluation includes ratings and justifications, but explicitly mapping each rating back to the rubric descriptors would deepen your product evaluation.

Your learning goal is clear and linked to personal interest, but it would be stronger if you specified a timeline or measurable milestones (e.g. âby week X I will draft a chapterâ), making it more actionable and time-bound.
Youâve identified key skills and outcomes for your product goal; adding a sequenced timeline of tasks and listing required resources would enhance the feasibility of your plan.
Your action plan table includes specific tasks, dates, durations and progress status, demonstrating thorough planning and clear sequencing of project steps.
Consider adding contingency measures to your action plan to address potential delays (e.g. exam conflicts) and maintain project momentum.
Your structured research plan with clear lines of inquiry, data sources and recording methods demonstrates strong application of ATL research skills.
You effectively applied research skills by consulting primary (librarian, teen authors) and secondary sources, using authentic examples to break down your learning goal.
Your use of open-ended emails and interviews to gather primary data shows excellent communication skills in engaging peers and refining your project.
Proactive use of a reminders app highlights strong self-management and organization, ensuring you stayed on track despite competing academic demands.
Your flexible timetable and prioritization of the first chapter (allocating 7 hours) demonstrate thoughtful organization and task management.
Using a traditional plot diagram to map exposition, climax and resolution shows strong critical-thinking skills in structuring your narrative.
Creative thinking is evident in your cover design conceptsâvisual metaphors (dark forest, solitary house, beam of light) effectively reinforce your storyâs theme.
You applied digital design principles wellâresponsive typography and A4 landscape layout improve readability and cross-device compatibility.
You demonstrate open-mindedness by actively seeking and incorporating peer feedback, showing adaptability and a willingness to improve.
Your critique of accessibility limitations (layout optimization, translation gaps) indicates strong information-literacy and critical evaluation skills.
